sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच
संन्यासस्य महाबाहो तत्त्वं च्छामि वेदितुम्।
त्यागस्य च हृषीकेश पृथक्केषिनिषूदन॥
transliteration:
arjuna uvāca
sannyāsasya mahā-bāho tattvam icchāmi veditum
 tyāgasya ca hṛṣīkeśa pṛthak-keśi-niṣūdana
literal_meaning: Arjuna said: O mighty-armed one, I wish to know the truth of sannyāsa and also of tyāga, O master of the senses, O slayer of the Keśī demon.
contextual_meaning: After receiving the entire teaching, Arjuna still feels a subtle cognitive-emotional split between two kinds of letting-go—formal monastic renunciation (sannyāsa) and inner psychological relinquishment (tyāga). He asks for final clarification so that doubt (symbolized as the demon Keśī) can be slain once and for all.
psychological_state: Pre-decisional cognitive dissonance—needing to collapse two similar constructs into one actionable path.
trigger_scenario: A high-achiever finishing an MBA contemplates whether to quit the corporate world (sannyāsa) or keep the job but detach from outcomes (tyāga).
anchor_text: True letting-go is inner surrender, not outer abandonment.
